subject: Sociology
title: Marx on Religion as Ideology
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Marx on Religion as Ideology.

hook.text: Religion is the opium of the people. Marx's famous line is often quoted but rarely understood in context.

buildup.text: Marx saw religion as a product of suffering. People create heavenly justice because earthly justice fails.

discovery.text: Religion dulls the pain of exploitation. It promises reward after death, discouraging revolt in life.

twist.text: Marx also called it the heart of a heartless world — he admitted religion offers genuine comfort.

climax.text: For Marx, ending inequality would end the need for religion. Fix the world and heaven becomes irrelevant.

quiz.question: Why did Marx call religion the 'opium of the people'?
options: He studied drug use in churches
